description: One-pass review of crate secretx-core (secretx-core/). Filed by /review-rusty-crate.
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — one P0 zeroization bug undermines the crate's entire stated security model (avkc.1); the rest are surface API decisions that fossilize at 1.0.
Correctness confidence: ~70% chance of missed bug. Least confident flags: avkc.1 (the zeroization-gap analysis assumes Rust's Vec/String reallocation semantics — needs to be confirmed by reading String::push and Vec growth behaviour, but the basic claim that String reallocation does not zeroize the old buffer is uncontroversial). Uncertainty driven by: I have not actually run a memory-scrub test (e.g. fill secret, drop SecretValue, scan freed pages); the leak is theoretical-but-likely. Would raise confidence with: a unit test that fills extract_field with a long secret, drops the result, then memcmp's known scratch buffers.
Idiom rating: ACCEPTABLE — code is correct and well-commented but has API-shape decisions (avkc.16, avkc.17, avkc.23) that fossilize at 1.0 and several mechanical clippy::pedantic issues that should be cleaned up before tagging. The two-scanner contract (lib.rs:121-160) is admirably documented but the byte/char halves have already drifted in idiom (is_some_and vs map().unwrap_or — see avkc.19) and pattern style (unnested or-patterns — see avkc.20).
Rust idiom confidence: ~75% chance of missed issue. Least confident flags: avkc.23 (factory takes &str) — there may be a reason the dispatcher hands the raw URI rather than parsed (e.g. to support future backends that want to do their own URI parsing for a non-standard scheme); needs to be discussed before fixing. Uncertainty driven by: I haven't read the umbrella secretx::from_uri dispatcher to see how it actually selects and calls the factory.
title: Review: secretx-core rusty pass
